# fucina

> A from-scratch Go + CUDA C++ inference engine for exactly one accelerator — the NVIDIA DGX
> Spark **GB10** (Blackwell, `sm_121a`, CUDA 13.0). Serves **Gemma 4 12B**, **Qwen3 / Qwen3.5 /
> Qwen3.6** (dense and MoE, GGUF/FP8-safetensors/NVFP4), and the experimental **DiffusionGemma
> 26B-A4B** text-diffusion MoE — all through one binary with server, one-shot, and REPL modes.
> Apache-2.0, **experimental, hardware-specific, no support**.
> *fucina* is Italian for **forge**. Repo: `github.com/hikmaai-io/fucina` (formerly `gem4d`).

This file orients an LLM/coding agent so it can build fucina, pick the right checkpoint, run
inference, call the API (including tool calling), and self-diagnose failures — without reading
the whole codebase first. Module path: `github.com/hikmaai-io/fucina`. Binary: `fucina`.

## Hard constraints (read before doing anything)

- **Hardware:** runs ONLY on the DGX Spark GB10 (`sm_121` / built as `sm_121a`). It will NOT build
  or run on other GPUs (RTX 30xx/40xx/50xx, A100, H100, B100/B200) — the Makefile pins `CUDA_ARCH`
  and the hot paths use GB10 FP8/NVFP4 tensor-core features. Do not attempt ports casually.
- **Toolchain:** CUDA 13.0 at `/usr/local/cuda-13`, Go 1.26 at `/usr/local/go` (override via
  `NVCC`/`CUDA_HOME`/`GO` make vars). `build-essential` (gcc/g++, make, ar).
- **Never commit/push model weights** — `*.gguf`, `*.safetensors`, `/models/` are git-ignored.
- **No model-select flag exists.** The architecture, quant format, and MoE-vs-dense shape are all
  detected from the model file/directory itself (GGUF metadata keys or `config.json` fields) —
  never invent a `--model-type`/`--arch` flag; it doesn't exist and isn't needed.

## Install & build (copy-paste)

```sh
sudo apt-get install -y build-essential
pip install -U "huggingface_hub[cli]"     # provides the `hf` command

make                       # dense Gemma/Qwen engine only
make CUTLASS_DIR=/path/to/cutlass   # add DiffusionGemma too (needs CUTLASS headers;
                                     # `pip install flashinfer` vendors them)
```
`make` compiles the CUDA static libs (`nvcc -arch=sm_121a`) then the Go binary, and asserts the
cubin arch + device-upload path are correct. Overridable vars: `NVCC`, `CUDA_HOME`, `GO`,
`CUTLASS_DIR`, `DG_GGUF`.

## Model support matrix (verified against the loader/detector code)

| Family | Detected from | On-disk format | Batching | Spec decode |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Gemma 4 12B** | GGUF fallback (no `qwen*` arch key) or generic NVFP4 safetensors | Q4_0-QAT / Q8_0 GGUF; NVFP4 safetensors (`RedHatAI/*`, `nvidia/*-FP4`) | opt-in (`--batch`) | prompt-lookup + MTP `--assistant` |
| **Qwen3 dense** | GGUF `general.architecture = "qwen3"` | GGUF (Q4_K_M etc.) | **mandatory (auto)** | prompt-lookup |
| **Qwen3 MoE** | GGUF `general.architecture = "qwen3moe"` | GGUF | **mandatory (auto)** | off (MoE) |
| **Qwen3.5 hybrid dense** | GGUF `general.architecture = "qwen35"`, or safetensors `config.json` `model_type` contains `"qwen3_5"` + `quant_method` `fp8`/`modelopt` (no expert tensors) | Q4_K_M GGUF; official FP8-block safetensors | **mandatory (auto)** | prompt-lookup |
| **Qwen3.5/3.6 MoE (A3B)** | same safetensors detection, `mlp.experts.0.gate_proj.weight` tensor present | official FP8-block safetensors; **nvidia ModelOpt mixed NVFP4/FP8** | **mandatory (auto)** | off (MoE) |
| **DiffusionGemma 26B-A4B** | separate engine, selected with `-dm` | Q4_K_M GGUF + NVFP4 MoE experts | N/A (block diffusion) | N/A |

"Qwen3.6" is a checkpoint generation name, not a separate architecture in code: `Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8`,
`Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`, and `nvidia/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-NVFP4` are all served through the same
`qwen3_5`/`qwen35` detector and loader as Qwen3.5 (same Gated-DeltaNet hybrid architecture). Don't
grep the source for a literal `"qwen36"` string — it isn't there.

**Continuous batching (paged KV + per-step scheduler) is auto-enabled and REQUIRED for every
Qwen3/3.5/3.6 checkpoint** — the single-flight prefill path is Gemma-only. `cmd/fucina/main.go`
detects this (`eng.IsQwen3Family()`) and turns batching on for you; a bare
`fucina -m <qwen-checkpoint>` just works. If paged-KV pool allocation fails you'll see:
`fucina: Qwen3 requires paged KV but the pools are not active (allocation failed — lower --ctx or
raise --gpu-mem-util)` — that's your fix.

## Run Qwen3.6 FP8 / NVFP4 (copy-paste)

```sh
# 35B-A3B MoE, official FP8 (DeepSeek-V3 block-quant)
hf download Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 --local-dir ./models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8
./fucina -m ./models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080

# 27B dense, official FP8
hf download Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 --local-dir ./models/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8
./fucina -m ./models/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080

# 35B-A3B MoE, NVIDIA ModelOpt MIXED_PRECISION (NVFP4 experts/shared-expert/lm_head,
# FP8 attention/GDN) — smaller footprint than the official FP8 build
hf download nvidia/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-NVFP4 --local-dir ./models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-NVFP4
./fucina -m ./models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-NVFP4 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080
```

One-shot and REPL work identically (both now route through the paged `SeqAdd`/`StepBatch` path,
not the Gemma single-flight path):

```sh
./fucina -m ./models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 -p "Write a haiku about CUDA." -n 100
./fucina -m ./models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 --interactive
```

`--batch`/`--paged-kv` and `FUCINA_BATCH`/`FUCINA_PAGED_KV` are no-ops to pass explicitly here —
they're already forced on. `--assistant` (Gemma-4 MTP draft head) does not apply to Qwen; leave it
unset for Qwen checkpoints.

**Perf defaults you get for free:** dense AND MoE Qwen3.5/3.6 checkpoints are requanted to Q4_K for
the attention/GDN mixer and FFN/expert weights at load (was MoE-only, now also dense) — smaller
resident weight set, faster decode. Escape hatch back to pure FP8: `FUCINA_MOE_FP8=1`.

## `-m` path resolution — the robust way (read this before debugging a load failure)

`-m` accepts: a `.gguf` file; a single `.safetensors` file; a directory containing
`model.safetensors.index.json` (sharded) or a lone `model.safetensors`, both needing a sibling
`config.json`. **It does not descend into a HuggingFace hub-cache's `snapshots/<hash>/` for you in
general** — with one exception:

- The Qwen3.5/3.6 FP8/ModelOpt loader specifically globs `<dir>/snapshots/*/model.safetensors.index.json`,
  so pointing `-m` at a raw hub-cache **repo root** (`models--Qwen--Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8/`, i.e. what
  `hf download <repo>` produces *without* `--local-dir`) works for **weight loading**.
- The Go-side tokenizer auto-discovery (`cmd/fucina/main.go: siblingTokenizerJSON`) has **no such
  glob** — it only looks for `tokenizer.json` directly inside `-m`'s directory. Pointed at a
  repo root, it will NOT find the `tokenizer.json` that actually lives under `snapshots/<hash>/`,
  and you'll get a tokenizer-init failure.
- The generic (non-Qwen) NVFP4 loader used for Gemma-4 (`RedHatAI/*`, `nvidia/*-FP4`) has **no
  repo-root resolution at all** — it needs the real snapshot-shaped directory.

**Robust rule for every safetensors checkpoint, Qwen or Gemma:** always download with
`hf download <repo> --local-dir <dir>` and point `-m` at `<dir>` directly. This gives you a flat
directory with `config.json` + shards + `tokenizer.json` at the top level, which satisfies both the
weight loader and the tokenizer auto-discovery uniformly — no `--tokenizer` needed, no
repo-root-vs-snapshot ambiguity. If you instead used the bare `hf download <repo>` cache layout,
pass the actual `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--.../snapshots/<hash>/` directory to `-m`, not the
`models--...` parent.

## Tokenizer rules

- **GGUF models never need `--tokenizer`** — the vocab is inline in the GGUF (Gemma-4, Qwen3,
  Qwen3.5 GGUF alike).
- **Safetensors/NVFP4/FP8 checkpoints need a `tokenizer.json`**, auto-discovered as a sibling of
  `-m` (see path-resolution rule above) or passed explicitly via `--tokenizer <tokenizer.json>`.
  `--tokenizer` also accepts a `.gguf` to source vocab from instead.
- Auto-discovery failure is fatal with a message telling you to pass `--tokenizer` — there is no
  silent fallback in any run mode.

## Server / one-shot / REPL (generic, all model families)

```sh
# OpenAI-compatible server
./fucina -m <model> --ctx 32768 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080
curl http://localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}],"stream":true}'

# Interactive REPL — /reset /stats /thinking LEVEL /quit
./fucina -m <model> --interactive

# One-shot
./fucina -m <model> -p "Write a haiku about CUDA." -n 100
```
Chat dialect (Gemma template vs Qwen ChatML/XML tool calls) is picked **automatically from the
loaded vocab** (`<|im_start|>` present → Qwen) — there is no `--dialect` flag.

## API requests / tool calling

The wire format is OpenAI-shaped for every model, regardless of dialect. Minimal tool-calling
request (verified against `internal/server/qwen_dialect_test.go`):

```sh
curl http://localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions -d '{
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "weather in Paris?"}],
  "tools": [{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
      "name": "get_weather",
      "description": "Get the weather",
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {"city": {"type": "string"}},
        "required": ["city"]
      }
    }
  }],
  "max_tokens": 64
}'
```
Response carries standard `tool_calls` (`choices[0].message.tool_calls[].function.{name,arguments}`,
`finish_reason: "tool_calls"`) even though Qwen internally emits Qwen3-Coder's XML
`<tool_call><function=NAME>...</function></tool_call>` form — the server parses it for you.
Force a specific tool: `"tool_choice": {"type":"function","function":{"name":"get_weather"}}`.

Reasoning/thinking: `"reasoning_effort": "low"|"medium"|"high"` or `"thinking": true/false` in the
request; response text appears in `choices[0].message.reasoning_content`. Works the same for Gemma
and Qwen.

**`response_format`/`json_schema` (structured output) does NOT work against any Qwen checkpoint.**
It's implemented (host-side constrained JSON decoding) but explicitly route-guarded off the
continuous-batching path with HTTP 501:
`{"error":{"message":"response_format (constrained JSON decoding) is not supported under continuous
batching; run without --batch","type":"unsupported_under_batching"}}`. Since Qwen is always forced
into batching, this always 501s for Qwen today; it works for Gemma-4 single-flight.

Endpoints: `POST /v1/chat/completions`, `POST /v1/completions`, `POST /v1/embeddings` (stub),
`GET /v1/models`, `GET /health` `/healthz`, `GET /readyz`, `GET /metrics`. `/v1/*` accept
`Authorization: Bearer <key>` when `--api-key`/`FUCINA_API_KEY` is set; health/metrics stay open.

## Diagnostics (exact messages, so you can grep for them)

| Symptom | Message | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| No model given | `error: no model specified. Use -m <model.gguf \| nvfp4-dir \| model.safetensors>` | pass `-m` |
| Bad/missing tokenizer | `fucina: tokenizer init failed: ...` / `auto-detected tokenizer %s is not usable: ... (pass --tokenizer ...)` | pass `--tokenizer <tokenizer.json\|gguf>` |
| Safetensors dir has no config/index | loader error from `cuda/safetensors.h` (`Model::open`) | you likely pointed at a hub-cache repo root instead of the snapshot dir — see path-resolution rule above |
| Unsupported quant/format | engine init fails with `fucina: engine init failed: ...` | only Q4_0-QAT/Q8_0/Q4_K GGUF, official FP8-block safetensors, and NVFP4 (generic or ModelOpt) are accepted; anything else is rejected at load |
| GPU OOM at load | `fucina: engine init failed: ...` (CUDA alloc failure surfaces through the same wrapper) | lower `--ctx`, raise `--gpu-mem-util` toward 1.0, or use a smaller/lower-quant checkpoint |
| Qwen paged-KV alloc fails | `fucina: Qwen3 requires paged KV but the pools are not active (allocation failed — lower --ctx or raise --gpu-mem-util)` | lower `--ctx` or raise `--gpu-mem-util` |
| `response_format` on Qwen | HTTP 501 `unsupported_under_batching` | expected; not supported for any Qwen checkpoint today |

Other useful env vars: `FUCINA_MOE_FP8=1` (disable the Q4_K mixer/FFN requant), `FUCINA_KV_NVFP4=1`
(NVFP4 KV, memory-only), `FUCINA_NO_PACKED=1` (drop the packed-Q4_0 decode copy, Gemma dense, −~7 GiB
VRAM), `FUCINA_PAGED_MAXCTX`/`FUCINA_PAGED_MAXSEQS` (paged-pool sizing overrides),
`FUCINA_DEBUG=1`/`--debug` (dumps requests to `/tmp/fucina_debug.log`).

## Hardware / memory notes

- GB10: 128 GB unified LPDDR5X, ~273 GB/s peak bandwidth. Decode is bandwidth-bound on weight
  bytes for both Gemma and Qwen — fewer resident bytes (lower quant, dropping the packed copy)
  is the lever that helps; scheduling/launch tweaks are not.
- `--gpu-mem-util` (default `0.90`) caps total device-memory use (weights + KV + scratch) as a
  fraction of the GPU's total memory; the engine auto-caps `--ctx` / drops optional weight copies
  to fit. No hardcoded minimum-VRAM number exists for the 35B-A3B MoE checkpoints — if it doesn't
  fit, lower `--ctx` or raise `--gpu-mem-util` first, then try a smaller checkpoint.
- Gemma-4 dense sliding-window KV is a capped ring (`FUCINA_SLIDING_RING`, default 8192 tokens),
  ctx-independent; only the 8 global layers scale with `--ctx`. This ring mechanism is Gemma-only.

## Testing

```sh
# CPU-only, no GPU/model required — this is what CI runs
go test ./internal/server/ ./internal/server/batch/ ./internal/tokenizer/ ./internal/sampler/ ./internal/chat/
make check      # go vet + the same pure-Go tests

# GPU smoke (requires the GB10 + a built engine)
make smoke      # build + raw-prompt run

# GPU correctness/perf gates (require the GB10 AND real downloaded checkpoints at
# hardcoded /opt/spark/models/... paths — override with MODEL=/QWEN35_MODEL=/etc.
# NOT runnable in CI or without hardware+weights on disk)
make gpu-gates          # Qwen3 dense/MoE parity + spec + prefix/suffix gates
make qwen35-batch-test  # Qwen3.5/3.6 paged batch + CUDA-graph decode gate
make bench              # batch==single self-test + greedy byte-identity + tok/s
```

## Architecture (key files)

- [cuda/gemma4_detect.h](cuda/gemma4_detect.h): GGUF architecture/format detection
  (`gemma4_detect_from_gguf` branches on `general.architecture` = `qwen3`/`qwen3moe`/`qwen35`/Gemma).
- [cuda/qwen35_fp8_loader.h](cuda/qwen35_fp8_loader.h): Qwen3.5/3.6 FP8-block + ModelOpt
  MIXED_PRECISION safetensors loader (dense and MoE, per-tensor FP8/NVFP4 scale-sibling sniffing).
- [cuda/nvfp4_loader.h](cuda/nvfp4_loader.h) / [cuda/safetensors.h](cuda/safetensors.h): generic
  (Gemma-4) NVFP4 safetensors path and the container parser both loaders share.
- [cuda/gemma4_kernels.cu](cuda/gemma4_kernels.cu): the entire forward pass for every arch —
  quantized GEMV/GEMM, FP8/NVFP4 attention, GDN (Gated-DeltaNet) mixer, MoE grouped-expert GEMM,
  RoPE, RMSNorm, sampling, CUDA graphs, paged multi-sequence batching, spec-verify.
- [internal/engine/cuda/bridge.go](internal/engine/cuda/bridge.go): cgo bridge
  (`NewEngine`, `Prefill`, `Decode`, `IsQwen3Family`, `SeqAdd`/`StepBatch`, …).
- [internal/chat/dialect.go](internal/chat/dialect.go), [internal/chat/qwen.go](internal/chat/qwen.go):
  chat template + tool-call dialect (`Gemma` vs `Qwen`), auto-selected from the vocab.
- [internal/server](internal/server): OpenAI-compatible HTTP server, `internal/server/batch`:
  the continuous-batching scheduler (burst-admission coalescing, per-seq spec gating).
- [internal/tokenizer](internal/tokenizer): Gemma SentencePiece + native HF BPE (`tokenizer.json`).
- [cmd/fucina](cmd/fucina): CLI entry point + run modes (`main.go`, `args.go`, `oneshot.go`,
  `repl.go`, `repl_paged.go`).
- DiffusionGemma (separate engine): [internal/engine/diffusion](internal/engine/diffusion),
  [cmd/fucina/diffusion.go](cmd/fucina/diffusion.go). See README "DiffusionGemma".

## More

- [README.md](README.md): full install, models, usage, HTTP API, hardware, status.
- [docs/qwen-models.md](docs/qwen-models.md): Qwen3/3.5/3.6 detection reference, checkpoint-path
  gotchas, official-FP8-vs-ModelOpt distinction, dialect/tool-calling detail, diagnostics.
- [docs/nvfp4-safetensors.md](docs/nvfp4-safetensors.md): Gemma-4 native NVFP4 schema/internals.
- [docs/continuous-batching.md](docs/continuous-batching.md): paged-KV/batching design + internals.
- [docs/qwen35-beat-vllm-plan.md](docs/qwen35-beat-vllm-plan.md): measured Qwen perf vs vLLM.

  [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) · [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md).
- Status: experimental, single-hardware (GB10), best-effort support only.
